query: This 18-file PR touches the auth subsystem, JWT signing, and session storage. I want an adversarial multi-perspective review -- security, correctness, performance, API design -- with conflicts surfaced and a confidence score.
Spawn a swarm of reviewers over this 12-file migration PR. I need adversarial perspectives and a P0/P1/P2 ranking.
this pr is touching 15 files in the database layer, including a schema migration, can you do the deterministic multi-reviewer thing with reconciliation?
Run a deep RLM swarm review on this 6-file change to the SSO integration. Make sure security and performance reviewers actually disagree if they should.
Adversarial review please -- 8 files, payment processing, I want every reviewer perspective and explicit reconciliation, not a single-pass summary.
I need a multi-role code review (security, correctness, performance, API design) on this 14-file refactor. Capability-routed via rlm_query.
Run the swarm review with a migration-safety reviewer added -- this is a SQL migration runner change spanning 9 files and going to prod next week.
Heavy review for this 25-file change: 4 standard reviewer roles + concurrency role. Reconciled output with a confidence score.
Tiny PR: I renamed a helper from `to_string` to `as_str` in one file. Quick review please.
Review this single-file change against our project style guide.
Decompose this 200-file analysis with RLM and produce per-crate summaries.
Trigger the meta-learning agent on bigbox overnight.
Ingest this session's findings into the Rust Engineer haystack.
Search my Rust notes for tokio cancellation patterns.
Audit this single function for security -- it's 30 lines, just one file.
Sandbox this python script -- I don't trust the input.
Generate a structural PR comment with a Mermaid diagram for this 4-file change.
Run the full pre-merge quality gate on this change -- verification + validation evidence.
What's the difference between code-review and structural-pr-review skills?
Edit src/main.rs line 100 to use anyhow::Result instead of Box<dyn Error>.
